title: Sustainable Travel Planning
description: Which travel choices reduce environmental impact while still providing culturally enriching experiences?
narrative: The user seeks a guide on eco-friendly travel decisions that balance reducing carbon footprints and supporting local communities. Relevance depends on documents that discuss low‑impact transportation options, sustainable accommodations, certifications like Green Travel, best practices for responsible tourism, and ways to engage authentically with local culture without commercial exploitation.

title: Urban Heat Remote Sensing
description: How can remote sensing data be used to quantify and analyze urban heat island intensity across heterogeneous city landscapes?
narrative: The user seeks methodological guidance for integrating satellite imagery, ground‑based temperature logs, and GIS in urban heat island research. Relevant documents should include detailed case studies, calibration techniques, data fusion approaches, and best‑practice recommendations, while excluding generic reviews, unrelated climate studies, or purely theoretical papers.

title: Compact SUV Space
description: What interior and cargo dimensions do compact SUVs offer for city living and weekend trips?
narrative: The user wants to compare compact SUVs that are small enough for narrow city streets and tight parking yet still offer generous head‑room, leg‑room, and cargo capacity for family outings. Relevant documents must provide interior dimensions (seat pitch, legroom, headroom), cargo space measurements (rear seat folded/unfolded, trunk volume), and overall vehicle size (length, width, height) to assess suitability for both urban commuting and weekend travel. Documents that only list exterior styling or engine specs without interior dimensional data are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Mental Health
description: How do remote work arrangements influence employee mental health outcomes across various sectors?
narrative: We are looking for empirical studies that compare remote versus on‑site employees that measure indicators such as stress, burnout, job satisfaction, and overall psychological well‑being. The research should involve sizable, sector‑diverse samples (e.g., technology, education, healthcare, manufacturing) and use statistically rigorous methods (e.g., longitudinal or quasi‑experimental designs). Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed journal articles, industry reports with audit data, and studies published from 2018 onward that account for confounding factors such as work hours, home environment, and organizational support.

title: Solar Shading & Battery Life
description: What is the impact of using solar window shading on the battery performance and longevity of a car in hot climates?
narrative: The user is a car owner living in a hot region who wants to keep the interior temperature down while also protecting the battery from excessive heat.  They expect answers that cover thermodynamic effects of sunshades (e.g., heat reduction percentages, UV blockage), how lower cabin temperatures influence alternator load, battery discharge rates, and overall lifespan.  Relevance should include empirical data or studies, comparisons of different shading products (film, polycarbonate veils, suction‑cup visors), and practical tips for maintaining battery health when using such shading.  Non‑relevant content (general cleaning tips, unrelated product listings, or vague battery maintenance advice without quantitative links to shading) should be excluded.

title: Mini‑Boats Eco‑Stoves
description: Which compact, wood‑burning stoves are best suited for small boats and cabins while meeting eco‑design standards?
narrative: The user is planning to heat a small watercraft or tiny cabin and wants a stove that is lightweight, occupies little space, can burn 20 cm logs, produces minimal smoke, and complies with contemporary eco‑design regulations (e.g., RT2022). Relevant documents should describe stove dimensions, power output (1–5 kW), fuel compatibility, emission ratings, safety features for marine use, and certification details.

title: Electric SUV Warranty
description: What warranty coverage can I expect for a new electric SUV in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed comparison of warranty terms, duration, components covered, and manufacturer differences for electric SUVs released in 2024. Relevant documents must describe standard and extended warranty policies, encompassed parts (battery, drivetrain, electronics), service terms, and any specific guarantees from major EV manufacturers. Exclude documents that discuss older models, non‑electric vehicles, or unrelated warranty topics.

title: Social Media Polarization
description: What are the main drivers of political polarization on social media among European users in the past decade?
narrative: The user seeks to identify and evaluate the key factors that contribute to increased political polarization of European audiences on social media platforms. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed articles, quantitative data studies, policy reports, and platform‑specific analyses (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, TikTok) focusing on 2010–2024. Criteria for relevance: European‑centric scope, evidence‑based arguments (surveys, network analyses, algorithmic scrutiny), discussion of content, user demographics, and platform design features that foster echo chambers and ideological segregation.

title: Solar Incentives Germany
description: What are the latest policy developments and incentives for residential solar installations in Germany?
narrative: The user seeks to understand recent German renewable energy regulations, subsidies, tax credits, and compliance issues for home solar projects; relevant documents must detail new laws, financial incentives, timeline milestones, and application procedures.

title: Green Roof Impact
description: What factors determine the environmental benefits of green roofs in urban areas?
narrative: The user wants to understand how green roofs reduce heat‑island effects, manage stormwater, improve energy efficiency, and enhance biodiversity in cities. Relevant documents should mention empirical studies, temperature reduction metrics, runoff volume data, cost‑benefit analyses, and case studies from different climate zones, while avoiding general marketing claims or purely theoretical proposals.

title: Crit'Air Application Process
description: I need comprehensive, up-to-date guidance on how to apply for a Crit'Air vignette for my electric vehicle in France, including eligible classes, fees, procedures, and any recent regulatory changes.
narrative: The user is looking for precise, authoritative information on the Crit'Air sticker system. They want to know which class applies to electric vehicles, the cost, the official application steps (online, by mail, or via service‑public.fr), any necessary documents, validity duration, and current legal references. They also want insight into recent updates such as changes to exclusion zones or new requirements for low‑emission vehicle compliance.

title: Rooftop Permaculture Design
description: What are the best practices for creating a sustainable rooftop garden using permaculture principles in temperate climates?
narrative: The user intends to convert a commercial building’s roof into a productive, low‑maintenance garden. They need comprehensive guidance on structural load limits, soil mix, water retention and reuse systems, plant zoning for native and pollinator‑friendly species, pest and disease management, and regulatory compliance. Relevance criteria include detailed design schematics, case studies from similar climates, cost‑efficiency analyses, and practical maintenance schedules for rooftop systems.

title: Pyramid Energy Theory
description: Seeking scholarly perspectives on the claims that ancient pyramids generated or harnessed energy that affects celestial phenomena.
narrative: The user wants credible academic sources that critically examine the hypothesis that pyramids functioned as energy capacitors or emitters and how this intersects with contemporary astrophysical or geophysical research. Relevance requires peer‑reviewed papers, books, or well‑cited conference proceedings that discuss the physics, archaeology, or historical records related to pyramid‑based energy claims; non‑academic popular or pseudoscientific material is excluded.

title: Field Audio Preservation
description: I’m looking for reliable methods to preserve field audio recordings for long-term research use.
narrative: The user is an ethnographer who regularly collects field audio in remote settings and wants to ensure that the recordings are preserved with high fidelity, appropriate metadata, and a robust archival strategy. Relevance criteria include guidance on digitization standards (e.g., sample rate, bit depth), compression choices (lossless vs lossy), metadata schemas (PBCore, Dublin Core), physical vs distributed storage solutions, and proven best‑practice guidelines from institutional repositories.

title: Corporate Escape Games
description: How can escape‑game mechanics be adapted to train employees in high‑stakes decision making?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based guidelines on scenario realism, role‑playing, puzzle design, and assessment of knowledge transfer, with a focus on corporate settings such as security or crisis response.

title: Vannes Hidden Gems
description: I want a personalized weekend itinerary in Vannes that highlights lesser‑known historical sites, local museums, and authentic Breton cuisine at family‑run eateries, while avoiding generic tourist spots.
narrative: The user seeks a nuanced, local‑centric plan: a two‑day schedule that prioritizes off‑the‑beaten‑path attractions, recommendations from residents, and nearby non‑chain restaurants, with relevance judged by authenticity, local insight, and umber of days covered.

title: Impact of Remote Work on Urban Commute
description: How has the rise of remote work altered commuting patterns and transportation infrastructure in major cities?
narrative: The user seeks evidence on changes in traffic volume, public transit ridership, and cost savings attributable to remote work, preferring recent studies, city datasets, and expert analyses that provide measurable comparisons over time and discuss policy implications.

title: Plastic Pollution
description: What is the effect of microplastics on coral reef ecosystems?
narrative: The user is a marine biology student aiming to write a literature review on how microplastic contamination impacts coral reef health. They seek peer‑reviewed journal articles, systematic reviews, and case studies published between 2015 and 2025 that provide quantitative data, experimental results, and ecological assessments, excluding general news reports or blog posts.

title: Green Roof Implementation
description: What are the effective design, installation, and maintenance strategies for green roofs in mid‑sized European cities to improve urban sustainability?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information that includes technical guidelines, material selections, irrigation systems, maintenance regimes, financial analysis, and case studies of successful green roof projects in European municipalities, with an emphasis on documents that provide empirical data, policy frameworks, and best‑practice recommendations for practitioners and planners.

title: Green Energy Choice
description: I want to compare cost, green certification, and incentives for different residential electricity suppliers.
narrative: The user is a homeowner looking to switch to a green electricity plan. They need to evaluate suppliers based on price per kWh, renewable origin certification, flexibility of contract terms, and any user rebates or incentives. Relevant documents must provide comparative cost data, supplier certification information, and details of incentive programs for residential customers.

title: Electric Vehicle Lease
description: What are the best long‑term leasing options for a new electric SUV in Germany, including mileage limits, monthly payments, and available incentives?
narrative: The user is searching for comprehensive lease information for a new electric SUV in Germany. They need to compare lease term, mileage caps, monthly cost, residual values, insurance coverage, maintenance plans, battery warranty, and any government subsidies or incentives. Relevant documents will contain detailed leasing contracts, financial terms, incentive policies, and comparison tables.

title: Small Island Resilience
description: How are climate‑induced disasters affecting the socio‑economic resilience of small island developing states, and what policy measures are most effective in mitigating these impacts?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly analyses, case studies, and comparative policy evaluations that connect the frequency and severity of climate‑related disasters (e.g., cyclones, sea‑level rise, extreme heat) with measured resilience outcomes (e.g., livelihood recovery, infrastructure robustness, social cohesion) in small island developing states. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, impact assessments, and evidence‑based policy recommendations. Content that discusses general climate science, non‑island contexts, or focuses solely on natural hazard description without linking to resilience or policy effectiveness should be considered irrelevant.

title: Santa Cruz Van Rental
description: I want to rent a van in Santa Cruz das Flores that can carry a bike, includes a child seat, is affordable, and allows pickup at the airport.
narrative: The user plans a family outing in Santa Cruz das Flores during late November and early December. They need a van capable of carrying a bicycle, equipped with a child seat, priced below a specified daily threshold, offering free cancellation, and available for pickup and return at the local airport. Relevance is judged by van capacity, bike storage, child seat availability, price, cancellation policy, pickup location, and suitable travel dates.

title: Municipal Energy Plan
description: What steps should a small municipality take to design a renewable energy policy that balances cost, sustainability, and community engagement?
narrative: The user is a local government official or policy maker seeking practical guidance on crafting a renewable energy policy for a small town. They are interested in best‑practice case studies, cost–benefit analyses, financing mechanisms, regulatory frameworks, and strategies for involving local residents. Relevant documents should include policy examples from similar municipalities, funding or grant options, stakeholder engagement methods, and impact assessments.

title: Urban Rooftop Greenhouse
description: How can I design a sustainable, energy‑efficient rooftop greenhouse in a high‑rise apartment building to grow leafy greens year‑round?
narrative: The user is an urban property manager wanting to add a year‑long produce system on a residential roof. Relevance requires sources that explain the technical design of rooftop greenhouses, including insulation, windbreaks, solar‑panel integration, water‑recirculation systems, and HVAC for climate control. The answer should also address local building codes, load‑bearing constraints, cost‑benefit analyses, and successful case studies in similar climates. Preference is given to documents that present detailed engineering schematics, sustainable material recommendations, and quantitative energy savings data.

title: Urban Green Housing
description: What criteria should I consider to identify a truly green apartment in a large city?
narrative: The user wants practical evidence of sustainability: certifications, material sourcing, energy performance, and community renewable initiatives, and seeks sources that provide concrete data or case studies rather than generic marketing statements.

title: Sustainable Marine Footwear
description: What peer‑reviewed research exists on biodegradable materials used in marine footwear for offshore applications?
narrative: The user is a researcher seeking scholarly evidence on how biodegradable polymers and recycled composites are applied to marine footwear such as boat shoes, deck shoes, and marine boots. Relevance is limited to documents published after 2015 that provide empirical performance data (waterproofing, durability, abrasion resistance) and environmental life‑cycle assessments specific to maritime use. Accepted sources include academic journals, conference proceedings, and technical reports from reputable manufacturers; grey literature that offers detailed testing or design data may also be considered if it meets the criteria above.

title: EV Battery Lifecycle
description: I need comprehensive data on electric vehicle battery life cycles, including capacity fade, charging performance, environmental impact, and replacement cost.
narrative: The user is evaluating possible EV purchases and wants up‑to‑date, science‑based metrics on battery longevity. Relevant documents should contain quantitative life‑cycle analyses, peer‑reviewed studies, industry reports, and technical datasheets that address capacity fade over time, charging rates, thermal management, CO₂ emissions, and projected replacement costs. Marketing brochures that only claim ‘long range’ or ‘fast charging’ without data, or documents that focus on vehicle styling or interior features, are considered irrelevant.

title: AI Mental Health
description: How can machine learning models be used to predict and mitigate depressive episodes in adolescents?
narrative: The user is looking for technical and empirical information on model architectures, feature engineering, evaluation protocols, and ethical safeguards specifically applied to adolescent depression forecasting. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed research papers, detailed technical reports, or comprehensive case studies, and should exclude general press releases, product advertisements, or anecdotal blogs.

title: Mafate Trail Comparison
description: Which of the main Mafate hiking routes offers the best balance of scenery, difficulty, and accessibility for a 2‑day trip?
narrative: The user intends to plan a weekend hike in Cirque de Mafate and is looking for an objective comparison of the top routes—Maïdo, Fond Mafate, and Bras des Merles—in terms of distance, elevation gain, average hiking time, lodging options, trail conditions, scenic highlights, and any regulatory restrictions. Relevance requires detailed, evidence‑based descriptions and comparative analysis; generic trail lists, unrelated car rental information, or user opinions without supporting data are not acceptable.

title: Eco‑Travel Budgeting
description: How can I plan a sustainable vacation within a limited budget?
narrative: The user is a traveler focused on reducing their carbon footprint while staying on a tight budget. Relevant information should explain low‑carbon travel options, green accommodation, public transportation or shared mobility, carbon‑impact calculators, cost‑saving tips, and travel guides for eco‑friendly yet affordable destinations. Content that only lists expensive eco‑hotels or generic travel advice without a clear budget or sustainability focus is considered irrelevant.

title: Dashcam Liability
description: How do dashcam records factor into establishing driver liability in French car accident cases?
narrative: The user seeks detailed insights into French legal precedents, admissibility criteria, evidence standards, and insurance company policies concerning dashcam footage used to prove or refute fault in road incidents.

title: Bordeaux Family Weekend
description: I plan a weekend trip to Bordeaux with my family and need child-friendly activities, suitable restaurants, lodging options, and public transport details.
narrative: The user seeks a curated itinerary for a family of four, requiring information on family-friendly attractions, parks, museums, child‑oriented restaurants, lodging that accepts families, public transport routes and schedules, and proximity to key sites. Relevance is judged by factual, up-to‑date information that helps plan a practical weekend visit.

title: Volunteer Recognition in French Football
description: Where can I find examples of initiatives that publicly acknowledge the dedication of volunteers in French football clubs, especially those in the Indre‑et‑Loire region, and how these programs are funded or coordinated?
narrative: The user is interested in concrete programs and events that celebrate volunteers’ contributions to local football clubs. Relevant documents should describe partnership projects (e.g., with Crédit Agricole), award ceremonies, financial incentives, social media recognition, or district‑level initiatives. Content that merely lists club activities, unrelated to volunteer acknowledgment, or outside the French football context should be considered irrelevant.

title: Renewable Energy Subsidies
description: What are the available subsidies for renewable energy installations in France and how can residents apply?
narrative: The user is a homeowner planning to install solar panels and is seeking detailed information on government‑backed subsidies, eligibility criteria, required documentation, application deadlines, and the agencies responsible for processing subsidies. Relevant documents must address subsidy programs, eligibility rules, application procedures, and contact points for assistance.

title: Biometric Smart Home Security
description: What biometric methods enhance smart home security, and how do they balance privacy, cost, and ease of integration?
narrative: The user seeks comparative information on facial, voice, fingerprint, and iris authentication for IoT home systems, focusing on deployment challenges, privacy safeguards, cost‑benefit analyses, and future trend indicators relevant to practical integration.

title: Urban Solar Retrofit
description: I want to find comprehensive guidelines for installing solar panels on existing apartment buildings, including permits, cost‑benefit analysis, and energy storage options.
narrative: User seeks detailed technical and regulatory information tailored to multi‑unit residential buildings, prioritizing sources that cover roofing constraints, leaseholder negotiations, and net metering policies. Relevance requires real‑world case studies, up‑to‑date permit requirements, and financial modeling of return on investment. Documents that focus only on home solar or generic installation will be considered less relevant.

title: Leek Health Benefits
description: What health benefits are associated with consuming leeks, particularly regarding cardiovascular and digestive well‑being?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based information on the health effects of leeks. Relevant documents should contain peer‑reviewed studies, clinical trials, meta‑analyses, or systematic reviews that examine leeks’ impact on heart health, blood pressure, cholesterol, digestion, or related physiological outcomes, while excluding recipes, culinary guides, or unrelated herbal folklore.

title: Budget Used Gaming Collection
description: Where can I find a curated list of high‑quality used video games under €20 with proven seller reputations?
narrative: The user wants to build a budget gaming library and seeks reputable online marketplaces offering used video games priced below €20. Relevant results should list platforms that provide seller ratings, condition guarantees, user reviews, and verified item descriptions, ensuring that the suggested titles are frequently praised for value and quality. Non‑used listings, anonymous sellers, or sites lacking buyer protection policies should be excluded.

title: Car Grant Eligibility
description: Which criteria qualify an unemployed young person for Pôle emploi's free grant to purchase or repair a second‑hand car?
narrative: The user wants precise information about the eligibility conditions, required documents, and application process for the "subsidie pour l’achat d’une voiture" offered by Pôle emploi. Relevant content must describe age limits, employment status, financial thresholds, the nature of the grant (not a loan), and steps to apply or receive the aid.

title: Multilingual Autism Support
description: Which evidence-based interventions effectively support early autism detection and intervention for infants and toddlers in households where multiple languages are spoken?
narrative: The user seeks research studies, systematic reviews, or guidelines that evaluate interventions designed for early childhood autism in multilingual contexts, focusing on infants and toddlers under five, considering both behavioral and technological strategies, and the outcomes measured for both developmental and linguistic proficiency.

title: Eco Lamp Fuels
description: Which sustainable or bio‑based fuels can safely replace petroleum products in household oil lamps?
narrative: The user wants a review of alternative fuels—such as vegetable oils, bio‑diesel, or specially formulated lamp oils—including practical safety tips, compatible wick options, and evidence from tested lamp performance. Relevance requires up‑to‑date sources that discuss fuel characteristics, safety guidelines, and real‑world usage outcomes.

title: Accessible Hiking Routes
description: Which Madeira hiking trails are suitable for people with limited mobility, detailing difficulty, equipment needs, and transport proximity?
narrative: The user wants a curated list of trails on Madeira that are low‑difficulty, have minimal elevation changes, clear markers, and are close to shuttle or public transport stops. Relevance requires explicit discussion of trail difficulty, required gear, safety features, and easy access to transport links for groups with mobility limitations.

title: Urban Homeschooling Guidelines
description: What are evidence‑based curriculum frameworks and best practices for families homeschooling children in dense urban settings?
narrative: The user is a parent looking for comprehensive, interdisciplinary curriculum plans that leverage the unique resources, constraints, and diversity of an urban environment, and wants actionable strategies for integrating local culture, technology, and community partnerships. Relevant documents should detail curriculum structures, pedagogical models, or case studies specific to urban homeschooling contexts.

title: Professional Security Contract
description: What are the eligibility criteria and benefits for the Contract for Professional Security (CSP) in France?
narrative: The user seeks legal and practical information about the CSP, including which employees are covered, employer obligations, remuneration details, cooling‑off period, and any associated allowances or training opportunities. Relevant documents should detail statutory provisions, administrative procedures, and recent changes to the scheme.
